title: Acrostichum ruta-muraria
taxonomy: Acrostichum ruta-muraria is a species of fern in the family Marattiales. It belongs to the genus Acrostichum, which is a group of large, tropical ferns. The Marattiales order is a group of ancient ferns that are mostly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant is also classified as a vascular plant due to its ability to transport water and nutrients throughout its body. Acrostichum ruta-muraria is an epiphytic plant that grows on other plants and trees.
uses: Acrostichum ruta-muraria is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory properties. It is also used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves are often applied topically to alleviate pain and reduce swelling. In traditional remedies, Acrostichum ruta-muraria is also used to treat digestive problems and respiratory issues.
